problem: Find the product of the greatest common divisor and the least common multiple of $100$ and $120.$

classified_questions.question: What is the definition of the greatest common divisor (gcd) of two numbers?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we find the prime factorization of a number and why is it important when finding the gcd and lcm?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you explain the process of finding the prime factorization of 100 and 120?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What are the common factors of 100 and 120 based on their prime factorizations?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we find the gcd using the common factors?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the definition of the least common multiple (lcm) of two numbers?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you explain the process of finding the lcm of 100 and 120 using their prime factorizations?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we find the lcm by multiplying the highest powers of all primes?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What is the relationship between the gcd and lcm of two numbers?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Why is the product of the gcd and lcm equal to the product of the original two numbers in this case? Is it always true? If so, why?
classified_questions.label: e
